title: Intercity economic ties amplify natural disasters spillovers in China
data_sources: China Industrial and Commercial Enterprises Database (enterprise registration records)

data_scope.city_count: not stated verbatim in available metadata
data_scope.disaster_data: GDIS (Rosvold & Buhaug 2021, Sci. Data), open access
data_scope.economic_linkage_data: firm-level investment ties from enterprise registration records, 2000-2020 (confidential)
data_scope.headline_finding: ignoring economic ties underestimates total economic losses by 52.5%-57.5%

core_conclusion: Natural-disaster losses propagate along intercity economic linkages, not just geographic proximity. Combining geocoded disaster data with firm-level cross-city investment records in spatial Durbin models with economic spatial weight matrices, the authors find that ignoring economic ties underestimates total losses by 52.5%-57.5%. High closeness-centrality and high-clustering cities amplify negative spillovers, and the effect intensifies as the investment network densifies over time.
why_it_matters: Quantifies a hidden, network-mediated channel of disaster loss comparable in size to the geographic channel; reframes resilience as a structural network property; shows risk is growing as networks densify; motivates coordinated cross-city disaster-resilience policy.
why_nature: Systems-level framing of cities as a coupled economic network; pairs a methodological contribution (economic spatial weight matrices in SDM) with a quantified headline mechanism; strongly policy-translatable to cross-jurisdictional resilience; transferable beyond the China case.
added_value_to_my_research: Couple economic spatial weights with MRIO supply-chain cascade -> coupled space-economy disaster model and multi-source fusion template
Learn spillover propagator as physics-informed graph operator -> nonlinear shock propagation for my spatiotemporal prediction tasks
Resilient-rewiring optimization over city network -> third resilience objective in my efficiency-equity facility optimization
Add mobility/patient-flow spatial weight channel -> disaster shocks through intercity healthcare-access and telehealth lanes
Explainable-ML / MGWR over spillover model -> attribute spatially non-stationary amplifiers, transferable to accessibility prediction
